name: Web App Testing — Playwright E2E Browser Tests
description: Use Playwright for end-to-end browser testing of critical user flows. Automate, don't rely on manual QA.

name: Verification Before Completion
description: Never mark a task done without verifying the fix actually works end-to-end. Tests passing is necessary but not sufficient.
template:
## Verification Before Completion

### The Rule
NEVER report a task complete without running the following verification steps. Tests passing alone is not sufficient.

### Verification Checklist
```
[ ] 1. Run the full test suite — all tests pass
[ ] 2. Run the specific test for this change — passes
[ ] 3. Manually exercise the changed code path in a real environment
[ ] 4. Check for regressions in related functionality
[ ] 5. Confirm the original problem/requirement is resolved
[ ] 6. No console errors in browser devtools (for UI changes)
[ ] 7. No new TypeScript errors (npm run tsc -- --noEmit)
```

### Common Failure Modes
- "Tests pass" but the UI is broken (test doesn't cover the changed path)
- Bug fixed in one place but moved to another
- Fix works in dev but not in production config
- Requirement partially met (happy path works, errors broken)

### Mandatory Verification Statement
Before marking done, confirm:
> "I ran [specific verification step] and observed [specific result]. The original issue is resolved."

### For AI-Assisted Development
AI models cannot verify UI without browser access. Always manually verify:
- UI renders correctly
- User interaction works as expected
- No visual regressions
- Accessibility (keyboard nav, screen reader)

### Checklist
- [ ] Test suite passes
- [ ] Original issue confirmed resolved (not just "code looks right")
- [ ] Regression check done
- [ ] Verification statement written in PR description
